commands.note: KILLED 2026-05-21 — n=22, net -0.76 SOL, drop_top3 -1.49 SOL. ZERO trailing_tp exits across all 22 trades — the TP=150 threshold was never reached. Strategy ran as 'trailing-TP-disabled but trailing-flag-enabled' which is functionally identical to base but with worse trade selection in the run window. Verdict on the trailing TP hypothesis for best-double universe: NULL — can't even test it because TP=150 is too far for this universe to hit. Lesson: trailing TP only matters if TP is reachable; for high-TP strategies the experiment requires lowering TP first.

KILLED 2026-05-21 — n=9, net -0.52 SOL, drop_top3 -1.13 SOL. 1 trailing_tp exit fired across 9 trades. Avg net -11.48% vs base v9shadow-velmono-dev's +22.11% on the same filter universe. 33pp avg gap on small sample, but the drop_top3 is decisively negative. The 1 trailing exit didn't rescue the cohort.

KILLED 2026-05-21 — n=17, net -0.85 SOL, drop_top3 -1.59 SOL. Same pattern as v26-best-double-ttp: ZERO trailing_tp exits across 17 trades. TP=150 too far for this universe to hit consistently. Median net -34.23% — the strategy is losing badly on the trades that DO fire. Confirms: trailing TP cannot rescue a universe that doesn't reach TP.

KILLED 2026-05-21 — n=125 (largest v26 sample), net -0.02 SOL, drop_top3 -2.46 SOL. Trailing TP IS firing here (10 trailing_tp exits at 8% rate, similar to base's 10.3% take_profit rate). But the avg per trade is -3pp worse than base v25-bot-excl-climbing (+2.90%). Top winners DID capture moonshots (+205%, +158%, +125% — much higher than static TP=100), BUT near-miss trailing exits cluster around +85-95% net which is BELOW static TP. The arithmetic mean drops because near-misses outnumber moonshots on this universe. drop_top3 -2.46 confirms the wins are outlier-driven; strip the 3 moonshots and the strategy bleeds. The 'near-miss failure mode' confirmed on the largest sample we have.
